mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, thereby supporting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative on average when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% over any 3-year rolling period, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.5pp over 3 months during an economic recovery.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) show strong growth while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains elevated, it suggests a nascent economic recovery where consumer demand is picking up before the labor market fully heals, creating an optimistic environment for equities and positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth is positive and UNRATE is above 7%
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative on average when RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE is above 7% over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation (CPIAUCSL), a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates aggressive monetary tightening to curb price pressures. This typically leads to higher discount rates and concerns about economic slowdown, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% in a month
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances during high inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust economic activity and corporate profitability, which provides a fundamental tailwind for equity markets, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative on average when INDPRO 3-month growth is positive over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a deteriorating labor market and potential recessionary pressures, which typically leads to a decline in corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where UNRATE rises by >0.3pp in a month.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) are declining while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising, it signals a tightening monetary policy impacting interest-rate sensitive sectors, which can lead to broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average is declining and FEDFUNDS is rising
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive on average when HOUST 3-month average is declining and FEDFUNDS is rising over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic weakness, a significant increase in GDP (GDPC1) growth, often driven by government stimulus, can stimulate demand and provide a floor for corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 (annualized) growth rate exceeds 3% for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during slow growth regimes.
